test_output_summary: 38/38 tests pass in tests/forge/test_stage_taxonomy.py covering StageClass enum (order, names, values, StrEnum), STAGE_PREREQUISITES (7 entries, exact Group B rows, types), CONSTITUTIONAL_STAGES (frozenset, contains PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW), PER_FEATURE_STAGES (frozenset, exactly the 4 per-feature stages), module docstring (FEAT-FORGE-007 ASSUM-001 + ADR-ARCH-026), and structural invariants (module path, __all__, no forge.pipeline.* sibling imports). Existing 67/67 tests in test_pipeline_lifecycle.py and test_contract_and_seam.py still pass after converting pipeline.py to pipeline/__init__.py. ruff check and black --check (target py312) both clean.
implementation_notes: Converted the existing top-level src/forge/pipeline.py module into a package by moving its contents to src/forge/pipeline/__init__.py — this preserves all `from forge.pipeline import ...` callers (BuildContext, FakeClock, PipelineLifecycleEmitter, State, attach_correlation_id) verified across tests/forge/test_pipeline_lifecycle.py and tests/forge/test_contract_and_seam.py. Then added the new declarative submodule src/forge/pipeline/stage_taxonomy.py exporting StageClass(StrEnum) with the eight Mode A stages in the dispatch order specified by FEAT-FORGE-007 ASSUM-001, plus STAGE_PREREQUISITES (dict with the seven Group B Scenario Outline rows), CONSTITUTIONAL_STAGES (frozenset containing only PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW per ADR-ARCH-026), and PER_FEATURE_STAGES (frozenset of FEATURE_SPEC, FEATURE_PLAN, AUTOBUILD, PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW). The module deliberately avoids any imports from sibling forge.pipeline.* submodules so it can be imported by all Wave 2–4 downstream guards/dispatchers/context-builders without forming a cycle (per task implementation note). String values use the dash-separated names from the .feature file so callers can round-trip with the feature spec without a translation table.
concerns: Converted src/forge/pipeline.py into a package src/forge/pipeline/__init__.py to make room for the new stage_taxonomy submodule. This is a structural change visible in `git status`, but is import-compatible: the test suite for pipeline lifecycle (67 tests) still passes unchanged. Reviewers should confirm this restructuring is acceptable before downstream MAG7 tasks add further submodules.

completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/pipeline/stage_taxonomy.py. To make room for the submodule, the existing top-level src/forge/pipeline.py was moved to src/forge/pipeline/__init__.py — this preserves every existing `from forge.pipeline import ...` import site (verified by re-running tests/forge/test_pipeline_lifecycle.py and tests/forge/test_contract_and_seam.py: 67/67 still pass). TestModuleStructure::test_module_path_is_forge_pipeline_stage_taxonomy asserts `stage_taxonomy.__name__ == 'forge.pipeline.stage_taxonomy'`.

Defined `class StageClass(StrEnum)` with the eight members in the exact specified order. String values use the dash-separated names from the feature file ('product-owner', 'architect', 'system-arch', 'system-design', 'feature-spec', 'feature-plan', 'autobuild', 'pull-request-review'). TestStageClassEnum::test_stageclass_is_strenum_subclass asserts the StrEnum base. TestStageClassEnum::test_stageclass_member_names_in_dispatch_order asserts the exact list and order. TestStageClassEnum::test_stageclass_string_values_match_feature_file is parametrized over all 8 members.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Exports `STAGE_PREREQUISITES: dict[StageClass, list[StageClass]]` with exactly seven entries matching the Group B Scenario Outline: `architect ← product-owner`, `system-arch ← architect`, `system-design ← system-arch`, `feature-spec ← system-design`, `feature-plan ← feature-spec` (per feature), `autobuild ← feature-plan` (per feature), `pull-request ← autobuild for every feature`
completion_promises.evidence: Defined STAGE_PREREQUISITES as a dict[StageClass, list[StageClass]] with exactly seven entries, one per Group B row. PRODUCT_OWNER (the entry stage) is intentionally absent so callers can use `stage in STAGE_PREREQUISITES` as a 'has prerequisites?' predicate. TestStagePrerequisites::test_stage_prerequisites_has_exactly_seven_entries enforces the count, ::test_product_owner_has_no_prerequisites_entry guards the entry-stage exclusion, and ::test_each_prerequisite_row_matches_group_b_scenario_outline is parametrized over all seven rows.

Defined `CONSTITUTIONAL_STAGES: frozenset[StageClass] = frozenset({StageClass.PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW})` per ADR-ARCH-026 (every Mode A run terminates at a constitutional pull-request review gate). TestConstitutionalStages::test_constitutional_stages_is_frozenset, ::test_constitutional_stages_contains_pull_request_review, and ::test_constitutional_stages_is_exactly_pull_request_review enforce both type and contents.

Defined `PER_FEATURE_STAGES: frozenset[StageClass]` containing exactly the four per-feature stages. TestPerFeatureStages::test_per_feature_stages_is_frozenset confirms the type. ::test_per_feature_stages_contains_each_per_feature_stage is parametrized over all 4 required members. ::test_per_feature_stages_excludes_pipeline_wide_stages confirms the four pipeline-wide stages (PRODUCT_OWNER, ARCHITECT, SYSTEM_ARCH, SYSTEM_DESIGN) are absent. ::test_per_feature_stages_is_exactly_the_four_per_feature_members asserts strict equality.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Module docstring references FEAT-FORGE-007 ASSUM-001 and ADR-ARCH-026 (constitutional rule)

TestModuleDocstring::test_module_docstring_references_feat_forge_007_assum_001 asserts both 'FEAT-FORGE-007' and 'ASSUM-001' are present; ::test_module_docstring_references_adr_arch_026 asserts 'ADR-ARCH-026' is present.
